style.styleSentence: A high-energy fusion of early-2000s rap-rock bounce and modern industrial production featuring a mid-tempo (100 BPM) groove driven by heavy live drums, distorted guitar stabs, sub-bass pressure, and aggressive turntable scratches with glitch edits and pitch drops.
style.vocalStyle: Anxious male rap verses alternating with explosive rock chorus vocals and tense inner-voice whispers

sections.text:
You asked why it feels like night today
I was the one who dimmed the room
You asked what wasn’t right today
I was already moving through you
Cut—cut—cut you

You asked why the night came early
I pulled the shade across the sky
You asked what felt wrong inside you
I was teaching fear to lie
You asked why your chest stayed tightened
I kept my hand around the thread
You didn’t know what stressed you first
I fed the pressure in your head
You know what the voice feels like
Because I learned how to speak low
In the back of every thought
Where the quiet things grow
I’m the face that you hold inside
I wake up when your eyes close
I watch every little lie
And laugh when your balance goes
I see everything
Every crack you hide within
When it’s time to sink or swim
I breathe right under your skin

You keep looking back
But I was never far behind
You keep hearing storms
Because I spin them in your mind

I know why you’re paranoid
I’m the shadow at your back
I know why your head keeps spinning
I’m the wind inside the static
I know why you hear me within
I’m the voice you can’t undo
I know why the face is near
I’m the paper that cut you

Cut you
Cut you
Paper that cut you
Still beneath
Still beneath
Still beneath your skin

You said you’ve got a face in you
I know, I carved it carefully
Pointing out mistakes to you
Was the way I made you carry me
You said we all have one inside
Now you’re finally speaking true
But the face you fear in everyone
Is learning tricks from you
You asked what set you off first
I was there before the spark
You asked why you can’t stand it
I was whispering in the dark
They act like you don’t measure up
So I sharpen every doubt
I turn every little comparison
Into something you can’t get out
Everybody has a face inside
A watcher in the wires
A witness when the truth gets bent
A laugh beneath the fire
So when they sink or swim
They’ll feel what you’ve been through
The face inside is watching them
The way I watched you too

You keep looking back
But I was never far behind
You keep hearing storms
Because I spin them in your mind

I know why you’re paranoid
I’m the shadow at your back
I know why your head keeps spinning
I’m the wind inside the static
I know why you hear me within
I’m the voice you can’t undo
I know why the face is near
I’m the paper that cut you

The cut is right beneath your skin
The cut is right beneath your skin
The cut is right beneath your skin

When the sun goes down
I’m the reason light feels guilty
When the sun goes down
I make the daylight turn against you
When the sun goes down
I’m the grin behind the evening
When the sun goes down
I make the mirror sing back to you
You asked who was watching
You were
You asked what was laughing
You were
You asked why it followed
You moved
You asked what was inside
Not you
You don’t sing me
I remix you

Cut you
Cut you
Paper that cut you

I know why you’re paranoid
I’m the shadow at your back
I know why your head keeps spinning
I’m the wind inside the static
I know why you hear me within
I’m the voice you can’t undo
I know why the face is near
I’m the paper that cut you
I know why you’re paranoid
I’m the warning in the room
I know why the light betrays
I was standing next to you
I know why you can’t stop hearing
What I buried deep within
I know why the face is near
I’m still beneath your skin

You asked why it feels like night today
Now you know
You asked what wasn’t right today
Now you know

Cut—cut—cut you
Good night
